Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Re: Plaintiff Thien Nguyen'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two Or, In The Alternative, Application For Relief Under C.C.P. 473(B) From Order Re: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-07-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Kelli A. Herzog, Esq. GROTEFELD HOFFMANN LLP 415.344.9670 kherzog@ghlaw-llp.com
Attorneys for Defendant AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.
No Other Appearances.

No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Re: Plaintiff Thien Nguyen'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two Or, In The Alternative, Application For Relief Under C.C.P. 473(B) From Order Re: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel

Defendant's motion for reconsideration under CCP sec. 1008 and alternative relief under CCP sec. 473 is granted. It appears to the satisfaction of the Court that defendant's counsel did not receive actual notice of plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to plaintiff's second request for production of documents. The Court finds that such lack of actual notice constitutes new and different facts and circumstances within the meaning of Section 1008 and surprise within the meaning of Section 473. Further, plaintiff has failed to oppose the application for Section 473 relief. Accordingly, relief from the Order dated April 13, 2024, and filed April 16, 2024, is warranted and such order is hereby vacated. Plaintiff is granted leave to re-notice and re-serve the motion to compel, subject to actual notice to defendant.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)

PLAINTIFF THIEN NGUYEN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two

Plaintiff's motion for an order striking Defendant's objections and compelling further responses and production of all responsive documents to Plaintiff's Request for Production of Documents, Set Two, Request Nos. 1 through 55, is unopposed and is granted. Plaintiff shall serve further responses and produce all responsive documents within ten calendar days from the date of order. Prevailing party shall submit a proposed order in Word format to the Temporary Judge at jameshfleming@gmail.com.
